news.headline: Form 144: RPID insider notifies sale of 10,000 Class A shares (option exercise)
news.summary: An RPID insider filed a Form 144 to sell 10,000 Class A shares obtained via option exercise, the latest in a string of small routine insider sales also dated June 10 (5,000 shares), June 22 (3,688 shares) and July 1 (6,312 shares), against roughly 41.7 million shares outstanding.

news.headline: Form 144: RPID insider lists 6,312 Class A shares for sale via option exercise
news.summary: A separate Rule 144 notice disclosed a planned sale of 6,312 Class A shares tied to an option exercise, continuing the pattern of small routine insider disposals seen through June and early July.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems shares slip to around $1.68-$1.70 in thin trading
news.summary: RPID traded roughly 0.6% lower intraday on elevated volume (about 163,000 shares vs. an average of 126,000), with coverage still citing KeyCorp's May 29 price-target cut to $5 from $8 as an overhang on sentiment.

news.headline: Form 144: RPID insider notice of proposed sale of 3,688 Class A shares
news.summary: Another routine Rule 144 filing disclosed a planned sale of 3,688 Class A shares via option exercise, part of the ongoing small insider-selling cadence following the May equity/warrant raise.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Announces Inducement Grant Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
news.summary: The company granted 60,000 restricted stock units to a newly hired employee as a material inducement to employment, vesting in three equal annual installments over three years.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (analyst-calendar estimate, not yet company-confirmed)
catalysts.detail: Earnings-calendar trackers (StockAnalysis.com, TradingView, WallStreetZen) currently list July 31, 2026 as the next report date, though as of July 19 the company had not yet issued its customary advance press release fixing the date (it announced the May 13 Q1 date about a week ahead of time, on May 6). Historically RPID has also reported Q2 in early-to-mid August (Aug 4 in 2023, Aug 12 in 2025). Watch the guided Growth Direct placement cadence toward the 30-38 unit FY26 target (6 placed in Q1), consumables pull-through and gross-margin progress toward the ~20% FY26 target, and cash runway following the May equity raise and subsequent sell-side price-target cuts.

pulse: RPID has been quiet operationally since the May dilution-heavy equity/warrant raise (~$9.8M upfront, up to $32M if warrants are exercised) and the June 10 inducement RSU grant, with no new system orders, partnerships or regulatory news disclosed in the last six weeks. The stock has stayed weak and choppy, trading roughly in the $1.68-$2.29 range through late June and mid-July on thin volume, still pressured by the May dilution and KeyBanc's/Lake Street's price-target cuts (to $5 and $7 from $8), while a steady drip of small Rule 144 insider option-exercise sales (June 10, June 22, July 1, July 8) has continued without signaling any change in the underlying story. The core automated microbial QC business is still executing toward FY26 guidance of 30-38 Growth Direct placements and $37-41M revenue (after 6 placements and 11% revenue growth in Q1), so the near-term focus is squarely on the still-unconfirmed Q2 2026 report, tentatively expected around July 31, for evidence that placement and consumables momentum can outrun continued cash burn and dilution risk.
